imitazioni.contexto: A very common expression of surprise or shock in English.
Informal way to greet someone and ask how they are.

imitazioni.frase_inglês: I'm afraid I can't make it.
imitazioni.frase_espanhol: Tengo miedo de que no pueda asistir.
imitazioni.contexto: A polite way to say you cannot attend something or do something.
Used to confirm that you understood something clearly.

imitazioni.frase_inglês: Could you say that again, please?
imitazioni.frase_espanhol: ¿Podrías decir eso otra vez, por favor?
imitazioni.contexto: Essential phrase when you didn't hear or understand something.

imitazioni.frase_inglês: It's on the tip of my tongue.
imitazioni.frase_espanhol: Está en la punta de mi lengua.
imitazioni.contexto: Used when you almost remember something but can't quite get it.
imitazioni.audio_ipa: /ɪts ɒn ðə tɪp əv maɪ tʌŋ/

imitazioni.frase_espanhol: No podría estar más de acuerdo.
imitazioni.contexto: A strong way to express complete agreement with someone.
Used to dismiss something or tell someone not to worry about it.
Idiomatic expression used to wish someone good luck before a performance or challenge.
A polite and emphatic way to confirm agreement.

imitazioni.frase_inglês: What do you do for a living?
imitazioni.frase_espanhol: ¿A qué te dedicas? / ¿Cuál es tu profesión?
imitazioni.contexto: Common question when getting to know someone new.
imitazioni.audio_ipa: /wɒt duː juː duː fər ə ˈlɪvɪŋ/

imitazioni.contexto: Said to someone you haven't seen for a long time.
